data.content:
Here's the full diagnosis of the `hard/` subfolder's MSBuild issues:

---

**MSBuild evaluation order** for `hard/HardExtension.csproj` (SDK-style):

1. SDK props → Microsoft.Common.props → auto-imports `hard/Directory.Build.props` (parent root one is **shadowed**, never imported)
2. Project file body
3. SDK targets → Microsoft.Common.targets → auto-imports `hard/Directory.Build.targets` (parent root one is **shadowed**)

---

**Issue 1 — Common.props never imported (circular condition)**
`hard/Directory.Build.props` line 9–10:
```xml
<Import Project="shared\Common.props"
    Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' == 'true'" />
```
The condition requires `CommonPropsImported` to already be `true`, but that property is **defined inside** `Common.props` (line 3). Classic chicken-and-egg — the import never fires. The condition should be `!= 'true'` (a guard against double-import).

**Issue 2 — Parent Directory.Build files never chained**
Because `hard/Directory.Build.props` exists, MSBuild stops walking upward. The root `Directory.Build.props` (which defines `RepoRoot`) is never imported. Same for `Directory.Build.targets`. Both `hard/` files need an explicit `GetPathOfFileAbove` import to chain to the parent.

**Issue 3 — `CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` set too late**
Both `hard/Directory.Build.targets` (line 3–5) and the root `Directory.Build.targets` (line 7–8) set `CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets`. This property is consumed **early** in `Microsoft.Common.targets` — *before* `Directory.Build.targets` is imported. By the time MSBuild reads these files, the hook point has already passed. This property must be set in `Directory.Build.props` to take effect. This is why the NuGet package's pre-build hook is silently dropped.

**Issue 4 — Missing file errors on fresh clone**
`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 8 imports `$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets` unconditionally. Since `Common.props` never loads (Issue 1), `RepoToolsPath` is undefined, producing a missing-file error. Similarly, the root `Directory.Build.targets` imports `$(RepoRoot)eng\custom-analyzers.props` — `RepoRoot` would be undefined for `hard/` projects even if chained, because it depends on the root props being loaded.

**Issue 5 — `GenerateAssemblyInfo` target name collision**
`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 11–13 defines a target named `GenerateAssemblyInfo`. The SDK already ships a target with that exact name (in `Microsoft.NET.GenerateAssemblyInfo.targets`). MSBuild's last-definition-wins rule means the custom target **replaces** the SDK's, silently breaking automatic assembly info generation.

**Issue 6 — `ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps` set too late**
`hard/Directory.Build.props` line 4 sets this to `false`, but the "Before" wildcard imports in `Microsoft.Common.props` have already been processed *before* `Directory.Build.props` is imported. Only the "After" setting on line 5 actually takes effect.

**Issue 7 — `_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal` is an internal target**
`hard/Directory.Build.targets` line 16 hooks `AfterTargets="_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal"`. The underscore prefix marks this as an internal/private target — it may be renamed or removed across SDK versions, making this fragile.

---

Now let me apply the fixes:

arguments.old_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Disable wildcard imports for controlled build behavior -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps>
    <ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared repository settings -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' == 'true'" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Chain to parent Directory.Build.props (root sets RepoRoot, etc.) -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <!-- Disable wildcard imports for controlled build behavior.
       Note: ImportByWildcardBeforeMicrosoftCommonProps cannot be disabled from
       Directory.Build.props because the "Before" wildcards are already processed
       before this file is imported. Only the "After" setting takes effect here. -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>false</ImportByWildcardAfterMicrosoftCommonProps>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Import shared repository settings (guard against double-import) -->
  <Import Project="shared\Common.props"
      Condition="'$(CommonPropsImported)' != 'true'" />

  <!-- Pre-build validation hook: must be set in props, not targets, because
       Microsoft.Common.targets consumes this property before importing
       Directory.Build.targets. -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)hooks\pre-build-validation.targets</CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>

arguments.new_str:
<Project>
  <!-- Chain to parent Directory.Build.targets -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.targets', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <!-- Import shared code analysis rules (guarded against missing path) -->
  <Import Project="$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets"
      Condition="'$(RepoToolsPath)' != '' and Exists('$(RepoToolsPath)\code-analysis.targets')" />

  <!-- Custom assembly info generation — renamed to avoid colliding with the
       SDK's built-in GenerateAssemblyInfo target, which would be silently replaced. -->
  <Target Name="GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <Message Text="Custom assembly info generation" Importance="high" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Run post-build checks. Uses the public CopyFilesToOutputDirectory target
       instead of the internal _CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal which may change across
       SDK versions. -->
  <Target Name="RunPostBuildChecks" AfterTargets="CopyFilesToOutputDirectory">
    <Exec Command="post-build-check.exe $(TargetPath)" />
  </Target>
</Project>

data.content:
All fixes are applied. Here's a summary of **7 issues found and fixed**:

| # | Issue | Root Cause | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|-----|
| 1 | **Common.props never imported** | Condition `== 'true'` checks a property *defined inside* the file being imported | Flipped to `!= 'true'` (double-import guard) |
| 2 | **Parent props/targets shadowed** | `hard/` Directory.Build files exist, so MSBuild stops walking upward — root files never load | Added `GetPathOfFileAbove` imports to chain to parent |
| 3 | **Pre-build hooks silently dropped** | `CustomBeforeMicrosoftCommonTargets` set in `.targets` — but consumed *before* Directory.Build.targets is imported | Moved to `Directory.Build.props` (both root and `hard/`) |
| 4 | **Missing file errors on fresh clone** | Unconditional `<Import>` of paths that may not exist (`RepoToolsPath`, `RepoRoot` + `eng\...`) | Added `Exists()` conditions |
| 5 | **SDK's `GenerateAssemblyInfo` replaced** | Custom target reused the SDK's exact target name — last-definition-wins silently replaced it | Renamed to `GenerateCustomAssemblyInfo` |
| 6 | **`ImportByWildcardBefore` ineffective** | Set in Directory.Build.props, but "Before" wildcards already processed by then | Removed; kept only the "After" setting that works |
| 7 | **Fragile `_CopyFilesMarkedCopyLocal` hook** | Internal underscore-prefixed target may change across SDK versions | Changed to public `CopyFilesToOutputDirectory` |
